summary: A soundboard capture with a bit of hiss delivers an early‑71 Keith era showcase, highlighted by a soaring Dark Star, fiery China>Rider and stellar playing from Jerry, Phil and the new keyboards.
review: The recording is a decent soundboard source – clear enough to enjoy despite some noticeable hiss. Musically the show shines with a 20‑minute Dark Star, a ripping China>Rider, and standout moments from Black Peter, Candyman and St. Stephen, all buoyed by Jerry’s lead work, Phil’s thumping bass and Keith’s fresh organ sound.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 detailed reviews consistently praise the performance (many 4‑5 stars) while noting only minor hiss issues, yielding a strong show rating and good recording quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear with a pleasant balance; reviewers note a persistent but not overwhelming hiss and occasional slight drop‑outs early in the set.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong, cohesive early‑71 set list that flows smoothly; the second set builds into a cosmic Dark Star jam and the band stays tightly locked throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring leads on Dark Star, aggressive jamming on Playin' and an overall crushing performance.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for thumping bass in China>Rider and high‑register licks that stood out as “otherworldly.”
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned for his humorous banter and added guitar work, especially on China>Rider.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s debut organ/piano described as a sweet honky‑tonk tone that injected fresh energy into the band.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy’s drumming called kick‑ass and solid, keeping the group tight.
